title: 617. Merge Two Binary Trees
question:
Given two binary trees and imagine that when you put one of them to cover the other, some nodes of the two trees are overlapped while the others are not.

You need to merge them into a new binary tree. The merge rule is that if two nodes overlap, then sum node values up as the new value of the merged node. Otherwise, the NOT null node will be used as the node of new tree.

Example 1:

Input: 
	Tree 1                     Tree 2                  
          1                         2                             
         / \                       / \                            
        3   2                     1   3                        
       /                           \   \                      
      5                             4   7                  
Output: 
Merged tree:
	     3
	    / \
	   4   5
	  / \   \ 
	 5   4   7

 

Note: The merging process must start from the root nodes of both trees.
code: # Definition for a binary tree node.\u000A# class TreeNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.left \u003D None\u000A#         self.right \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def mergeTrees(self, t1, t2):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type t1: TreeNode\u000A        :type t2: TreeNode\u000A        :rtype: TreeNode\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        def _merge_nodes(node1, node2):\u000A            if not (node1 and node2): return node1 or node2\u000A            node1.val +\u003D node2.val\u000A            node1.left \u003D _merge_nodes(node1.left, node2.left)\u000A            node1.right \u003D _merge_nodes(node1.right, node2.right)\u000A            return node1\u000A        return _merge_nodes(t1, t2)\u000A
